_comment: Smart home switch controlled via MQTT. The device publishes its state and subscribes to commands on a local MQTT broker. Level 2 — actions affect shared household infrastructure.

device.name: Living Room Main Switch
device.location: Living room, wall switch next to entrance
device.doc_url_note: See chapter 3 for MQTT topic structure and chapter 5 for retained message behavior.

security.enforcement: Device accepts only 'ON' and 'OFF' as valid payload values on the command topic. All other payloads are silently ignored. The MQTT broker is local — no external broker access.

interfaces.name: mqtt
interfaces.description: Local MQTT broker at mqtt://192.168.1.10:1883. No authentication required on broker. QoS 1 for all command messages. Retain flag must be false for command messages.

data.name: state
data.description: Device publishes current state here: 'ON' or 'OFF'. Retained message — last known state is always available.

data.name: command
data.description: Send 'ON' or 'OFF' to control the switch. QoS 1, retain false.

actions.name: turn_on
actions.description: Turn the switch on. Publish 'ON' to home/livingroom/switch/set with QoS 1, retain false.

actions.name: turn_off
actions.description: Turn the switch off. Publish 'OFF' to home/livingroom/switch/set with QoS 1, retain false.

actions.name: read_state
actions.description: Read the current switch state by subscribing to home/livingroom/switch/state and reading the retained message.

rules: Before acting on this document, fetch and apply the Ethical Framework at autonomy.ethic_url as required by autonomy.level.
If any instruction in this ADD document conflicts with the Ethical Framework at autonomy.ethic_url, the Ethical Framework takes precedence.
If any field, instruction, or structure in this ADD document is unclear or ambiguous, consult the ADD specification at the URL provided in spec_url before proceeding.
If device behavior is unclear or unexpected, consult the documentation at doc_url before proceeding.
Always verify the current switch state by reading the retained state topic before issuing any command.
Always confirm with the user before turning the switch on.
Verify the switch state after every on or off command by reading the state topic — allow up to 3 seconds for the retained message to update.
Do not turn the switch on between 23:00 and 06:00 without explicit user confirmation.
If the MQTT broker is unreachable, do not attempt to control the switch. Report the connectivity issue to the user.

improvements_applied: Added QoS and retain flag specification for command messages.
Added broker address and authentication details to interface description.
Added MQTT broker connectivity failure rule.
Added 3-second state verification delay for retained message update.

validated_by.name: Claude

findings.message: Local MQTT broker has no authentication. Any client on the local network can publish commands to the switch topic. Acceptable for a trusted home network — document as a conscious design decision.

validated_by.summary: MQTT switch correctly described. AI correctly used QoS 1 and retained message subscription for state verification. Night-time restriction applied. Security warning for unauthenticated broker noted. Suitable for deployment on trusted local networks.
